fields.description:
## Context

Band-A (A3) auto-remediation drove SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT to zero on the shared store but, by
design, LEFT the order-sensitive, human-triage faults for manual reconciliation. As of
2026-07-11 `rebar fsck` reports **17** such faults: **13 ORPHAN_EVENT** (pre-snapshot EDIT
events absent from the snapshot's `source_event_uuids`) + **4 MISSING_CREATE** (110e, 957d,
9fa4, cf5d). Auto-repair is unsafe here: `rebar fsck --repair-snapshots` rebuilds from the full
log and previously CORRUPTED 11/13 statuses, and `--repair` routes EDIT orphans to human triage
(won't fix them). The store has been backed up (188MB git bundle + 967-ticket NDJSON export at
HEAD 8c3c26f72) before any mutation.

## Forensic classification (recorded in detail as a comment on this ticket)

- **13 ORPHAN_EVENT** — benign snapshot-staleness from a distributed compaction-vs-reconciler
  write race; live status/assignee verified correct for all 13 (each orphan's effect is a
  redundant duplicate of an identical post-snapshot reconciler EDIT).
- **4 MISSING_CREATE** — local-only phantom dirs (0 tracked files, not in HEAD, no structural
  references). 957d/9fa4/cf5d are empty; 110e's `.cache.json` holds an epic that is a duplicate
  of the already-tracked open epic 6d0d (content not lost).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] [operator-attested] Per-orphan disposition applied: retire each of the 13 orphan EDIT files (`.json` ->
  `.json.retired`, which fsck's `is_active_event()` skips) — lossless, touches no status.
- [ ] [operator-attested] MISSING_CREATE disposition applied: delete the 4 local phantom dirs (110e resolved as a
  duplicate of tracked epic 6d0d; 957d/9fa4/cf5d are empty residue).
- [ ] [operator-attested] `rebar fsck` reports zero ORPHAN_EVENT + zero MISSING_CREATE, and no ticket's displayed
  status/assignee changed (verified via a before/after diff of all 13 orphan tickets — all
  `closed`; assignees `""` except cc5b and f112 = "Joe Oakhart").
- [ ] [operator-attested] Decisions recorded on the ticket (done — see the forensic comment) and the store change
  committed to the `tickets` branch and pushed.

## Notes

- Root cause (compaction snapshotting before observing a remote reconciler write) is a recurring
  race; a durable fix is separate follow-up work, out of scope for this remediate-to-zero ticket.
